water_temp_read.read: Water temperature is in a favorable trout range.

light_pressure_read.read: Light and pressure do not strongly push the outlook either way.

stocking_read.read: Recent stocking supports eggs, worms, and attractor nymphs.

angler_analysis.expected_trout_behavior: Expect trout to favor the most comfortable feeding lanes while staying close to cover when light is high.
angler_analysis.presentation_style: Prioritize clean drifts, smaller natural patterns, and stocked-trout attractors where appropriate.

fly_reasoning.egg_pattern: Recent stocking makes eggs a logical confidence fly.
fly_reasoning.squirmy_worm: A simple stocked-trout attractor and good dirty-water option.
fly_reasoning.duracell: A strong attractor nymph when fish will move a little but are not fully aggressive.
fly_reasoning.zebra_midge: Small and natural for clear, pressured, technical conditions.
fly_reasoning.pheasant_tail: A reliable natural nymph when trout are feeding but cautious.

generated_text.quick_read: Trout fishing outlook for the Chattahoochee River is very good with ideal water temperatures and recent stocking enhancing opportunities. Expect favorable conditions for various nymph and midge presentations.
generated_text.best_window: The morning of day 2 is the best window because water temperature is ideal and conditions support effective use of nymphs, midges, soft hackles, and opportunistic streamers in productive trout positions.
generated_text.current_conditions: USGS reports water temperature at 55.6°F, flow at 991 cfs, gage height of 2.18 ft, and turbidity measured at 6.4 NTU, indicating clear conditions.
generated_text.weather_setup: Sky cover is light averaging 19%, pressure is steady at 30.05 inHg with a neutral trend, wind from the west at 5.6 mph average with gusts up to 17.3 mph, and minimal rain expected (0.02 inches with up to 27% chance).
generated_text.what_this_means: Trout will favor comfortable feeding lanes near riffles, runs, seams, and structure. Use clean drifts with smaller natural patterns and stocked-trout attractors like egg patterns, squirmy worms, and duracell flies. Balanced nymph approaches and midges will also be effective. No cautions from pressure trends or light were noted.
